channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.rockpapershotgun.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.rockpapershotgun.com

item.title: Death Stranding 2's PC launch comes with a tougher new mode and, staying true to the brand, live-action cutscenes
item.content:encoded:
Plus some other smaller bits

That there PC release of Death Stranding 2: On the Beach is now only a couple of days away, and with that Kojima Productions et al have decided to grace us with those last few details about the new stuff that'll be coming alongside its launch. If you're just interested in whether you can run it on your rig or not, well, luckily the system requirements were released last month, so you're already sorted there.

On to the new stuff! For starters, according to this handy PlayStation Blog post, a new "to the wilder," (decapitalisation theirs, not mine) difficulty mode will be available to have a go at. It's one for those after a challenge, with the only details provided about it being that there will be "deadly enemies" and that "completing orders will require every ounce of ingenuity you can muster."

You'll also be able to don Sam with some bandanas that don't quite Solid Snakeify him that can be acquired through completing some new VR training missions that see you repeating fights against Neil (the guy who is definitely just Snake again). George Miller's Chiral Feline may also now appear in the private room on the DHV Magellan, including in the game's photo mode.

Weirdly, one detail that was omitted from the blog post, but apparently mentioned in Hideo Kojima's podcast Spacewalk (thanks MP1st and Okami) is the fact that there will be live action cutscenes that were originally cut due to capacity limits. I await the "Kojima just wants to make a movie" comments with not particularly baited breath.

Death Stranding 2: On the Beach will be getting its hiking boots on later this week when it launches on PC on March 19th.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/death-stranding-2s-pc-launch-comes-with-a-tougher-new-mode-and-staying-true-to-the-brand-live-action-cutscenes
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The very Rimwold-esque Going Medieval hits 1.0 today after almost five years of early access
item.content:encoded:
Oh you're going there? Just take the next left

The time is upon us! The time, that is, to go medieval through Going Medieval, the very Rimworld-like colony sim and city builder, as after about five years in early access, the game's 1.0 launch has arrived. Despite all those years worth of updates, there's actually a lot that's arrived in this one, so let's look at some of the highlights.

Top of the order are the additions of global stats and grand objects. Starting with the latter, grand objects are essentially the end point of a game of Going Medieval. As soon as you've picked one, you're stuck with it and it alone, so you may want to play around a bit before locking yourself in. The way this feature works is that "influence is taken out of the game and region map and replaced with global stats. Global stats are these global counters that are increased and decreased by your actions. These stats are hidden until you reach a certain threshold." There's six stats total, one of which is a secret, teehee!

Once you've maxed out a stat, that's when you can pick a grand objective, which might include a couple of religious-themed ones, a university oriented one, and once again, a secret one! Teehee! And once you've beaten these sort-of quests, that's it, the game has been beaten (until you decide to do another run).

Those of you completely new to Going Medieval that find it a bit intimidating may be interested to know that the game's tutorial has had a big revamp making it more accessible to, well, everybody. There are new starting conditions too, which mostly relate to how difficult a time you do or don't want, with some more granular modifiers should you choose to engage.

This is only really the tip of the iceberg when it comes to the 1.0 additions however, so if you want a fuller picture you can have a gander at this Steam post right here. And if you fancy giving it a go, well, Going Medieval is out in 1.0 on Steam right now, and it's got a launch discount that's running until the end of the month.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/the-very-rimwold-esque-going-medieval-hits-10-today-after-almost-five-years-of-early-access
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
